definitions: To express a desire for something to happen, often in a ritualistic way (e.g., before blowing out candles on a birthday cake or seeing a shooting star).
To hope for a particular outcome.
examples: She closed her eyes to make a wish.
Don't forget to make a wish when you see a falling star.
He made a wish for world peace.
pastTenses: made a wish (simple past), made a wish (present perfect), made a wish (past perfect)
